title: RV8GR whole-system chip-level virtual test
purpose: Collect the tested chip-level RV8GR blocks into one virtual whole-system gate while physical build evidence is pending.

chips.role: RAM store/load and branch marker path
SETDP, SETPG, and J page behavior
EI, DI inert behavior, IRQ release latch

wiring.rule: Stress clock/reset timing virtually, but do not claim physical edge quality.
Reset release must keep known boot state.
Exactly one active IBUS driver per checked phase.
Exactly one active DBUS driver per checked phase.
Architectural checkpoints must match lower-level trace final states.

behavior.coverage_rule: This package references existing executable circuit proofs instead of duplicating their detailed vector logic.
behavior.chip_level_rule: All 18 RV8GR chips must remain present in the RV8GR doc virtual bench plan with five split-record mappings each.
behavior.system_rule: Boot, Lab 13 marker, RAM store/load/branch, page jump, IRQ latch, and bus ownership proofs must all be part of the gate.
behavior.noise_rule: RCParasitic and DelayNoise are applied only as virtual stress instruments; physical signoff remains blocked.

timing.frequency_boundary: Functional and virtual stress only; physical voltage/frequency/scope evidence is still required.
timing.student_rule: If the whole-system virtual test fails, debug the named lower-level package before touching the real build.

guarantees: all 18 RV8GR chips have virtual bench mappings
boot sequence and Lab 13 marker traces are included
critical nets have RCParasitic and DelayNoise stress
hardware signoff remains blocked until physical evidence exists

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
